customer_anxiety_profile: Gary is not anxious in the traditional sense — he is mildly frustrated. Ion missed the prior appointment and called two days late. He rebooked without resistance but his cooperation is transactional, not enthusiastic. The closer should expect a customer who is giving Ion a second chance, not a warm lead.
unresolved_concerns: Prior missed appointment — Ion failed to call on the scheduled night and followed up two days late. Gary named this explicitly. No trust-repair move was made beyond a quick apology.
Fiancée on file — her number is in the system but it was not confirmed whether she should be on the appointment. Co-decision-maker status is unresolved.
sensitive_topics_raised: Ion's prior service failure — Gary raised it twice. Do not minimize or re-apologize without a concrete action attached.
exact_promises_rep_made: Phone call Monday at 6PM on Gary's number (not fiancée's number).
Email with appointment details will be sent.
closer_should_know: Gary rebooked after Ion missed the prior appointment. He is cooperative but the trust deficit is real and unaddressed. Open with a direct acknowledgment of the prior miss and a specific action that makes Monday different — then move to the design. Do not treat this as a warm inbound; treat it as a second-chance call where the first 60 seconds either rebuild or lose the trust.
